Warm-Up Science

Stretching is making you slower.

Before tennis, the classic jog-and-hold is quietly costing you pace.
Swipe
AMIRARDEKANI.COM

Cold muscle = slow serve.

— Late first step · stiff serve · sluggish reactions
AMIRARDEKANI.COM

Not all stretches are warm-ups.

— Static hold
  • Muscle goes loose
  • Serve slows down
  • First step is later
  • Feels productive
→ Dynamic move
  • Joints primed
  • Serve speed up
  • Reactions sharper
  • Same flexibility
AMIRARDEKANI.COM

Warm up in four steps.

01
Raise
JOG · SHUFFLE · 3–5 MIN
02
Activate & mobilise
SWINGS · LUNGES · 7–10 MIN
03
Potentiate
10S PRESS × 2
04
Start — don't wait
GO STRAIGHT IN
AMIRARDEKANI.COM
+4.6
km/h on the serve, from one 10-second press before serving.
Source: Isometric Conditioning in Tennis Players
AMIRARDEKANI.COM
The one rule

Move, don't hold.

Before you play
Move — dynamic only
01
After you play
Hold — static stretch
02
Long break mid-match
2-min re-raise
03
